question_title: How many Nintendo Switch 2 units will Famitsu report sold in Japan during the week of May 18–24, 2026?
summary: Forecasting Switch 2 Japan sales for May 18-24, 2026 - the final full week before a 20% price hike (¥10,000 increase effective May 25). This is a supply-constrained scenario: demand is effectively uncapped (massive panic buying with "launch-like hysteria" reported), but retailers are selling out and implementing restrictions. My estimate centers around 128,000 units based on a scenario mixture: (1) supply depleted at 15% weight, (2) moderate supply at 40%, (3) strong resupply at 30%, (4) exceptional throughput at 15%. The Golden Week period (~100k/week) serves as a floor for elevated-demand conditions, while post-launch weeks 2-3 (140-153k) establish an upper bound for sustained weekly throughput. Key uncertainty is the unobserved May 11-17 inventory depletion.

factors.description: Golden Week period (before price hike fully kicked in) already showed ~93-107k/week, establishing a floor for elevated demand conditions
Price hike urgency (LAST week before 20% increase) creates extreme demand; reports of 'hysteria', 'reminiscent of launch', long lines, and sell-outs at major retailers
Supply constraint: retailers implementing purchase restrictions (Bic Camera credit card only), sell-outs reported, inventory depletion from May 11-17 panic buying week
Post-launch weeks 2-3 (153k, 140k) establish upper bound for sustained weekly Japan throughput when Nintendo was actively prioritizing supply
Short supply chain (China/Vietnam to Japan = 2-5 days), giving Nintendo ~10 days from announcement to redirect inventory; but not enough time for new production orders

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary: Nintendo Switch 2 Japan Sales (May 18-24, 2026)

**Median estimate: ~128,000 units** | 80% CI: [73,500 – 181,100]

### Key Reasoning

This is fundamentally a **supply-constrained** problem. Demand is essentially uncapped — everyone planning to buy a Switch 2 in the next year has an urgent incentive to buy this week (the last week before a 20% / ¥10,000 price increase). The real question is: **how many units can Nintendo get onto Japanese shelves?**

**Evidence supporting higher sales (~130-175k):**
- Golden Week already showed ~100k/week before the panic fully kicked in
- Reports describe "launch-like hysteria" with long lines at retailers
- Post-launch weeks 2-3 show Nintendo CAN deliver 140-153k units/week to Japan
- Short supply chain (China/Vietnam → Japan in days) allows some inventory redirection

**Evidence supporting lower sales (~70-100k):**
- Sell-outs reported at major retailers before this week even started
- Bic Camera restricting purchases to credit card holders (supply rationing)
- May 11-17 (first full panic week) likely already depleted much channel inventory
- Nintendo only had ~10 days from announcement to redirect supply — no time for new production

### Scenario Mixture
| Scenario | Weight | Mode |
|----------|--------|------|
| Supply severely depleted | 15% | 70k |
| Moderate supply constraint | 40% | 115k |
| Strong resupply | 30% | 145k |
| Exceptional surge | 15% | 185k |
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) How many Nintendo Switch 2 units will Famitsu report sold in Japan during the week of May 18–24, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43535)

[Switch 2 price increase in Japan causes hysteria, as stores restrict console sales](https://www.notebookcheck.net/Switch-2-price-increase-in-Japan-causes-hysteria-as-stores-restrict-console-sales.1293250.0.html)
[Some Japanese Retailers Are Now Heavily Restricting Nintendo Switch 2 Purchases](https://www.thegamer.com/switch-2-nintendo-japan-price-hike-retailers/)
resolution_criteria:
This question resolves as the number of Nintendo Switch 2 hardware units (summed across all variants sold in Japan, including the Japan-only model) reported by Famitsu as having sold in Japan during the week of May 18, 2026 through May 24, 2026, as reposted in Gematsu's weekly "Famitsu Sales" article covering that week. Report the value as a count of units (e.g., a reported value of 125,000 units resolves as 125000).

The primary source is Gematsu's Famitsu Sales tag page: https://www.gematsu.com/tag/famitsu-sales — specifically the article covering the 5/18/26 – 5/24/26 week. If Gematsu has not published the relevant article by May 31, 2026, the figure may be sourced from any other outlet that explicitly attributes the number to Famitsu's official weekly hardware chart for that week (e.g., Famiboards, Nintendo Life, or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)).
fine_print: If Famitsu reports separate sub-totals for individual Switch 2 SKUs/variants, the resolution value is the sum across all Switch 2 variants. If no Famitsu figure for the May 18–24, 2026 week is available from any credible source by May 31, 2026, the question resolves Ambiguous.

factors.description: Golden Week period (before price hike fully kicked in) already showed ~93-107k/week, establishing a floor for elevated demand conditions
Price hike urgency (LAST week before 20% increase) creates extreme demand; reports of 'hysteria', 'reminiscent of launch', long lines, and sell-outs
Supply constraint: retailers implementing purchase restrictions (Bic Camera credit card only), sell-outs reported, inventory depletion from May 11-17 panic buying week
Post-launch weeks 2-3 (153k, 140k) establish upper bound for sustained weekly Japan throughput when Nintendo was actively prioritizing supply
Short supply chain (China/Vietnam to Japan = 2-5 days), giving Nintendo ~10 days from announcement to redirect inventory; but not enough time for new production

input.anchor: Golden Week period average of ~100k/week as baseline for elevated-demand period, before price hike urgency fully manifested
input.assessment:
The May 18-24 week represents a unique confluence: the absolute last week before a 20% price hike on a hugely popular gaming console, with confirmed reports of panic buying and sell-outs. Demand is essentially uncapped (everyone who was planning to buy in the next year wants to buy THIS week). The binding constraint is supply.

Key supply analysis:
- Normal Japan throughput: ~45-60k/week
- Golden Week elevated throughput: ~93-107k/week
- Maximum sustained throughput (post-launch): 140-153k/week
- Time from announcement to this week: 10 days (enough for logistics redirect, not for new production)

The most likely range is 100-150k, with the median around 128k. The distribution is slightly right-skewed because the ceiling (post-launch maximums) is further from the median than the floor (supply depletion). 

Key uncertainties: We don't have the May 11-17 data yet - that week was the first full week of panic buying and would tell us a lot about how much inventory was burned through. Sell-out reports suggest some depletion, but retailers implementing restrictions (rather than saying 'sold out') suggests stock isn't completely gone.
input.calibration_notes: My P10-P90 range of 74k-181k spans roughly 2.5x. This seems appropriate given: (a) we have no data on the May 11-17 week, (b) supply dynamics are hard to predict, (c) post-launch weeks show 140-153k is achievable but not guaranteed. The median of 128k is meaningfully above the Golden Week average (~100k) but below the post-launch maximums (140-153k), reflecting that supply is constrained but demand pull is very strong.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. How much inventory remains after the May 11-17 panic buying week? 2. Did Nintendo successfully redirect supply to Japan in the 10 days since announcement? 3. Are sell-out reports widespread or limited to a few high-profile retailers?
input.tool_audit:
- research() provided excellent Famitsu historical data and price hike details
- web_search found critical launch week data (947k launch, 153k/140k weeks 2-3) and retailer restriction reports
- fetch_url confirmed retailer restrictions (Bic Camera credit card requirement, Yodobashi tactics)
- May 11-17 Famitsu data NOT YET PUBLISHED - this is a key data gap
- No prediction market data found for this specific question
input.process_reflection: The forecasting tools worked well for this question. research() provided comprehensive structured data. The scenario mixture approach with Monte Carlo feels appropriate for this supply-constrained question where the key uncertainty (inventory levels going into the week) is genuinely unobservable from public data. One limitation: I couldn't find any leaked/early data for the May 11-17 week, which would significantly narrow my uncertainty.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

### Resolution Criteria
Resolution criteria are clearly stated and the agent fetched the Metaculus question page, confirming bounds [0, 250000] and the Gematsu/Famitsu source requirement. The factors and assessment correctly target the Famitsu-reported figure for the May 18-24 week. No issues.

### Factor-by-Factor Verification

**Factor 1 (Golden Week baseline ~93-107k/week, logit=+1.0):** Grounded. The trace shows Famitsu data of 214,438 combined for Apr 27-May 10. The question background attributes the ~93k/~96k weekly split to VGChartz/Famitsu summaries, and the research tool computed the ~107k/week average. The claimed range of 93-107k spans both interpretations. Logit sign correct (pushes estimate up from lower baseline).

**Factor 2 (Price hike urgency, logit=+1.5):** Grounded. Notebookcheck article confirmed "hysteria," "reminiscent of its launch," "jam-packed" retailers. TheGamer article confirmed long lines and rush buying. Bic Camera credit card restriction confirmed by both sources. The 20% increase (¥10,000 on ¥49,980) confirmed by Nintendo's official announcement and multiple outlets. Logit sign correct.

**Factor 3 (Supply constraint, logit=-1.0):** Grounded. Bic Camera credit card restriction and sell-out reports confirmed by Notebookcheck and TheGamer fetch_url results. Logit sign correct (constrains estimate downward).

**Factor 4 (Post-launch weeks 2-3 as upper bound, logit=+0.5):** Grounded. Week 2 = 153,205 and Week 3 = 140,026 confirmed from nintendoeverything.com search results in the trace. Logit sign correct (establishes higher achievable throughput).

**Factor 5 (Short supply chain, logit=+0.3):** This is the agent's own logistics reasoning (China/Vietnam to Japan in 2-5 days). No external source directly confirms this timeline, but it's reasonable inference rather than a factual claim. The core conclusion—that Nintendo has some ability to redirect inventory within the 10-day window—is sound. Logit sign correct.

### Double-Counting Check
No double-counting found. Factors address different aspects: Factor 1 (baseline demand level), Factor 2 (demand urgency), Factor 3 (supply constraint), Factor 4 (throughput ceiling), Factor 5 (supply chain flexibility). Each draws on distinct evidence.

### Wrong-Direction Check
All logit signs are consistent with factor descriptions. Factors 1, 2, 4, 5 push the estimate up; Factor 3 pushes it down. This matches the narrative.

### Assessment Consistency
The assessment's median of ~128k is consistent with the net positive factor direction from an anchor of ~100k. The factors collectively push up from the Golden Week baseline (100k anchor) by roughly the right amount given the strong demand urgency (+1.5) partially offset by supply constraints (-1.0).

### Counterargument Quality
The counterargument is substantive and well-constructed. It identifies five specific reasons the estimate could be too high: (1) inventory depletion before the target week, (2) May 11-17 already exhausting channel inventory, (3) Nintendo's misaligned incentives (preferring to sell at higher price), (4) Golden Week tourism comparison being misleading, and (5) post-launch weeks having months of prep vs. 10 days. This is a genuinely strong alternative story, not a token gesture.

### Confidence Self-Assessment
Confidence of 0.40 is appropriate given: (a) key missing data (May 11-17 week not yet published), (b) genuine uncertainty about inventory levels, (c) no historical precedent for exactly this scenario. This reflects honest uncertainty.

### Probability Assessment
My independent estimate based on the evidence: median ~120-130k, P10 ~70-80k, P90 ~170-185k. The agent's center of 128k with range 74k-181k aligns well with what the evidence supports. The Monte Carlo approach with scenario weighting (15% severe depletion / 40% moderate / 30% strong resupply / 15% exceptional) is a reasonable decomposition. No meaningful divergence from my estimate.

### Anchor Divergence
The anchor is Golden Week ~100k/week. The final estimate is 128k, a departure of ~28k (~28%). This is justified by the strong Factor 2 (price hike urgency, logit=+1.5) which is well-supported by multiple confirmed reports of panic buying and retailer restrictions.

### Tool Audit
Agent's tool audit claims match the ground truth metrics: 15 calls, 0 errors, correct tool counts per type. No discrepancies.

### Worldview Consistency
No Nintendo/Switch 2/Famitsu entries found in the worldview store, so no cross-question consistency issues to flag.

### Minor Note
One source (nintendoeverything) mentions the price hike date as "May 26" while all others say May 25. This is a single-source discrepancy that doesn't affect the forecast since the question explicitly frames May 18-24 as the final week before the hike.

**Overall:** All factual claims are grounded in the trace. The estimate is well-calibrated, the uncertainty is appropriately wide, the counterargument is strong, and the self-assessed confidence is reasonable. No errors found.
